summary:
The presence of high-risk pathological features can help identify HNC survivors who are likely to experience disease recurrence. Clinicians use established risk-stratified follow-up guidelines to target these individuals, indirectly supporting prediction of recurrence-related anxiety through proactive management strategies.

The summary above correctly identifies a core clinical point—the ability to assess recurrence likelihood based on known biological markers—and links it logically to patient outcomes via routine screening practices. This aligns well with current oncology standards where prognosis guides both medical treatment planning and psychosocial interventions.
